_meta.description: Month-by-month 1980 economic + political indicators, aligned to a t=0 anchor (start of Persian Gulf supply crisis). Designed to overlay on 2026 trajectory so the user can read off 'we're at 1980 month X' in real time.
_meta.anchor_event_1980: 1979-11-04: Iran hostage crisis begins (start of sustained Persian Gulf risk premium phase)
_meta.anchor_event_2026: 2026-02-28: US-Israel airstrikes on Iran begin (start of current crisis)
_meta.alignment_method: Calendar months from anchor. t=0 is the start month of the crisis. Use to read 'X months in, Y was the value'.
_meta.warning: 1980 numbers are best-effort historical reconstructions from BLS/EIA/Gallup archives. Some months interpolated. Do not quote any single number without verifying against original source.

indicators_by_month.events: Iran hostage crisis begins. Initial rally effect lifts Carter +18pp.
Soviet invasion of Afghanistan. Oil hits $26.
Inflation peaks. Volcker tightens. Recession begins.
Desert One rescue mission fails. Approval collapse.
Carter wins primary fight against Kennedy. Reagan locks GOP nomination.
Dem convention — Carter renominated. Polls Reagan +5.
Iran-Iraq war starts (22 Sep). Oil briefly spikes.
Reagan-Carter debate (28 Oct). 'Are you better off?'
Election day (4 Nov). Reagan wins 489-49 EC, 50.7-41.0 popular vote.

alignment_with_2026.rationale: 2026-05 = 2.5 months after 2026-02-28 anchor. Roughly aligned with 1980-01 (t=2 since 1979-11 anchor).

next_milestones.2026-11 midterm: t=8 (analogous to 1980-07: GOP convention era). 1980 ICS was 58 — bouncing back from April trough; 2026 ICS already at 53 with no recovery sign.
next_milestones.2027-04: t=14 (post-1980 election analog). 1980 had Carter loss already locked in; 2027 inflation dynamics still TBD.
next_milestones.2028-11 presidential: t=33 (no 1980 analog — 33 months past anchor would be August 1982). Use scenario engine.

key_differences_to_track: 1980 had Q1 (-1.6%) AND Q2 (-7.9%) negative GDP growth before election. 2026 hasn't yet had a quarter print negative.
1980 had peak CPI 14.8% (April). 2026 CPI is 3.3% — much lower despite ICS being similar.
1980 ICS bottom was 51 (May/June). 2026 ICS is 53 in May — same level but with 1/4 the inflation. This is the perception-data divergence Stantcheva 2024 documents.
1980 incumbent (Carter) was running for re-election. 2026 has Trump as incumbent for midterm but Trump is term-limited for 2028 — open seat.
1980 had no rally event near election. 2026 stands at ~2 months post-rally (Iran strikes Feb 28); rally has likely decayed.

lessons: 1980 trajectory: ICS bottomed in May (t=6) at 51, then partially recovered to 64-68 by election (t=11-12). Despite recovery, voters still punished incumbent — perception scarring durable. Implication for 2026: ICS at 53 in May will likely partially recover by November but not fully.
1980 oil prices peaked April (t=5, $39) and gradually drifted lower by election ($37). Compare 2026: April peak $128, May $100. Trajectory faster but starting from much higher base.

Carter's approval only briefly rose with rally events (Iran hostage rally Nov-Dec 1979 = +18pp; primary win June 1980 = +4pp). All decayed within 2-4 months. Implication: Trump's Iran strike rally (if it gave him +5-8pp in March) will be largely gone by November midterms.

Q2 GDP (Fair model's main G input) was -7.9% in 1980 → catastrophic. Even with Hormuz crisis, 2028 Q2 GDP unlikely to be that bad in any of our 4 scenarios. Suggests Fair model will read 'less bad than 1980' for 2028 — this is correct directionally but may understate political damage.
